e2e.runner_script: app/scripts/run-e2e.mjs (spawns next dev on random port)

test_runners.typecheck: implicit via next build; no standalone tsc target

paradigm: layered/n-tier with package-by-feature inside components and reactions; React Context (MarmotContext) is the integration seam between UI and marmot-ts/IDB/NDK

naming_conventions.rumor_kind_constants: exported from the owning sync module (e.g. PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND from profileSync.ts)

module_map_relevant_to_epic.epic_change: Sign in serialiseProfileUpdate (becomes async, takes signer); verify embedded SignedProfileEvent in parseProfilePayload; thread signedEvent through payloadToMemberProfile; legacy fallback for unsigned payloads

exports: constants (PROFILE_STALENESS_MS, REQUEST_DEDUPE_MS, UNANSWERED_RETRY_MS, UNANSWERED_MAX_ATTEMPTS, RELAY_BACKOFF_MIN_MS, RELAY_BACKOFF_MAX_MS)

module_map_relevant_to_epic.owns: module-level pending-relay timer Map keyed by (groupId, targetPubkey)

module_map_relevant_to_epic.epic_change: Add quizzl-profile-request-memos store (pattern from joinRequestStorage); add memo CRUD (loadProfileRequestMemo, saveProfileRequestMemo, recordRequestEmitted, recordRequestAnswered, clearProfileRequestMemos); persist signedEvent in mergeMemberProfile; wire clearProfileRequestMemos into clearAllGroupData

Insert PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND arm in onApplicationMessage dispatcher (after kind 7 reaction arm, ~line 680); verify SignedProfileEvent and call notifyProfileObserved in PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND arm; add app-start sweep useEffect (gate ready && groups.length>0 && pubkeyHex); expose requestProfilesIfStale on context value; remove proactive on-member-add republish (current lines 709-716); call clearProfileRequestMemos from leaveGroup (current lines 954-969)
Export SignedProfileEvent type; add signedEvent?: SignedProfileEvent to MemberProfile
Inside GroupDetailView's route-enter useEffect (lines 120-173), call requestProfilesIfStale(id) after getMemberProfiles

module_map_relevant_to_epic.purity: pure factory; methods are async I/O
exports: createPrivateKeySigner -> EventSigner with getPublicKey, signEvent (finalizeEvent), nip44
module_map_relevant_to_epic.epic_change: None — reused by serialiseProfileUpdate to sign the embedded kind:0 SignedProfileEvent

spec_line_reference_drift.explanation: Spec was written against an older revision of MarmotContext.tsx; the file has grown. Architects should rely on architecture.md module map and re-grep landmarks before editing.

spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.issue: serialiseProfileUpdate becomes async and takes signer
callers_to_update: MarmotContext.tsx:710 (proactive republish on member-add — being removed anyway)
spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.note: This is a function-signature break the spec mentions only in the file-by-file table; architect must update every caller. Verify call sites are still in the same place when implementing.

spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.issue: leaveGroup is in MarmotContext.tsx, not groupStorage.ts
spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.spec_text: wire clearProfileRequestMemos into clearAllGroupData and leaveGroup
spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.actual: clearAllGroupData lives in groupStorage.ts (line 182); leaveGroup is a useCallback inside MarmotContext.tsx (lines 954-969). Both touchpoints must be wired.
spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.spec_file_table_attribution: Spec attributes both wire-ups to groupStorage.ts; reality requires touching MarmotContext.tsx as well.

spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.background: MIP-03 dictates inner rumors are unsigned. marmot-ts does not surface MLS sender leaf to consumers, so receivers trust rumor.pubkey without verification. The embedded-signature mechanism is what makes relay-on-behalf safe and incidentally hardens first-hand profile broadcasts; other rumor kinds (chat, score, polls, reactions) keep current trust model.
spec_gaps_and_call_site_breaks.decision: Out of scope for this epic — tracked separately in marmot-ts-rumor-sender-authentication.md

patterns: buildRumor + sendRumorSafe + mlsGroup.sendApplicationRumor — every outbound rumor goes through this triple inside MarmotContext
Pure sync module (constants + serialise/parse/payloadTo*) mirrors profileSync.ts / pollSync.ts / scoreSync.ts
IDB store created at module top via idb-keyval createStore('<db-name>', '<object-store-name>') — one DB per logical domain
Version counter bumped after IDB write resolves, preventing stale-read races (setProfileVersion etc.)
Dynamic import of marmot-ts/NDK/idb-keyval inside async functions to keep top-level safe for SSR

test_infrastructure_gaps.needed_for: Scenario: per-peer dedupe (count outbound PROFILE_REQUEST_KIND and inbound PROFILE_RUMOR_KIND from a peer)
test_infrastructure_gaps.approach: page.addInitScript installs a global window.__rumorCounters; instrument onApplicationMessage and sendApplicationRumor to bump counts; expose getRumorCount(page, kind, direction) helper

test_infrastructure_gaps.needed_for: Scenario: clear C's IDB profile entry for B without wiping all state
Scenarios: periodic refresh (8d ago), retry state machine (1h boundaries, 7d window)
test_infrastructure_gaps.approach: Use Playwright's native page.clock.install + page.clock.fastForward (Playwright 1.45+); the project is on 1.58 so this is available. For unit tests use vi.useFakeTimers (already established).

boundary_invariants: MarmotContext.tsx is the only module that calls sendRumorSafe and mlsGroup.sendApplicationRumor
groupStorage.ts (and the few standalone *Storage.ts modules) are the only modules that call createStore for group-related data
profileSync.ts is the only place ProfilePayload is parsed (+ co-located new profileRequestSync.ts for ProfileRequestPayload)
Signer comes from MarmotContext via createPrivateKeySigner; it is not constructed in lib helpers
NDK is always accessed via getNdk() from ndkClient.ts singleton
important_constraints: Static export deployment (output: 'export') — pages must use query params, not [id].tsx (CLAUDE.md)
Multi-platform development (mac arm + linux x86) — all build/test goes through make to handle platform stamp
All user-visible UI text via useCopy() from LanguageContext; never hardcoded; both en and de translations required
MIP-03: outer MLS rumor stays unsigned (no sig); embedded signature lives inside rumor.content
Backward compat: legacy peers emitting flat ProfilePayload must still merge (signedEvent: undefined, non-relayable)
